QUESTION_TEXT: What is a default argument in C++ function? Write a function Area() with one default argument.
TOPIC: C++ Basics - Reference Parameters and Default Arguments
ANSWER_TEXT: A default argument provides a default value if no value is passed. Example: float Area(float l, float b=5.0){ return l*b; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function using #define to find the cube of a number. Show its usage in main().
ANSWER_TEXT:
#define CUBE(x) (x*x*x)
void main(){ int n=3; cout<<CUBE(n); }  Output: 27

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the output of: int arr[]={5,10,15,20}; int *p=arr; cout<<*p<<' '<<*(p+2)<<' '<<p[3];
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: 5 15 20

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of function overloading in C++ with an example showing two functions named calculate().
ANSWER_TEXT: Function overloading allows multiple functions with the same name but different parameters. Example: int calculate(int a, int b){ return a+b; } float calculate(float a, float b){ return a*b; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ program to find the largest element in a 1D array of n integers using a function.
ANSWER_TEXT: int largest(int arr[], int n){ int max=arr[0]; for(int i=1;i<n;i++) if(arr[i]>max) max=arr[i]; return max; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to calculate the sum of each row of a 2D array of size 3x3.
ANSWER_TEXT: void rowSum(int a[][3], int r){ for(int i=0;i<r;i++){ int s=0; for(int j=0;j<3;j++) s+=a[i][j]; cout<<'Row '<<i<<' sum='<<s<<endl; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the address stored in pointer p? int arr[5]={10,20,30,40,50}; int *p=arr+2; cout<<*p;
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: 30 (p points to arr[2] which is 30)

QUESTION_TEXT: What will be output of: for(int i=0;i<3;i++){ for(int j=i;j<3;j++) cout<<'*'; cout<<endl; }
Explain the use of random() function in C++ and write a statement to generate a random number between 1 and 100.
ANSWER_TEXT: random() generates a pseudo-random number. Statement: int r = random(100)+1;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is encapsulation in C++? How is it achieved?
ANSWER_TEXT: Encapsulation is the binding of data and functions together. It is achieved using classes where data members are private and accessed through public member functions.

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between a constructor and destructor in C++ with examples.
ANSWER_TEXT: Constructor: initializes object on creation (e.g., Test(){ x=0; }). Destructor: cleans up on object destruction (e.g., ~Test(){ cout<<"Destroyed"; }).

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ class STUDENT with private members RollNo and Name, and public members to input and display data.
ANSWER_TEXT: class STUDENT{ int RollNo; char Name[30]; public: void GetData(){ cin>>RollNo>>Name; } void ShowData(){ cout<<RollNo<<' '<<Name; } };

QUESTION_TEXT: What is inheritance? List the types of inheritance supported in C++ with one-line descriptions.
ANSWER_TEXT: Inheritance allows a derived class to reuse properties of base class. Types: Single, Multiple, Multilevel, Hierarchical, Hybrid inheritance.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ class hierarchy showing single inheritance: class Vehicle as base and class Car as derived. Include one data member each.
ANSWER_TEXT: class Vehicle{ protected: int speed; public: void setSpeed(int s){ speed=s; } }; class Car:public Vehicle{ int doors; public: void setDoors(int d){ doors=d; } void show(){ cout<<speed<<' '<<doors; } };

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a copy constructor? Write one for a class BOOK with a single integer data member Pages.
ANSWER_TEXT: BOOK(BOOK &b){ Pages=b.Pages; } Copy constructor creates a new object as a copy of an existing object.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the significance of the scope resolution operator (::) in C++?
ANSWER_TEXT: The :: operator is used to define member functions outside the class and to access global variables when a local variable has the same name.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the output: class A{ public: A(){ cout<<'A'; } ~A(){ cout<<'~A'; } }; void main(){ A obj1, obj2; }
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: AA~A~A (constructors called in order, destructors in reverse)

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between public and private access specifiers with an example in C++.
ANSWER_TEXT: Private members cannot be accessed from outside the class. Public members can be accessed from anywhere. Example: class X{ int priv; public: int pub; };

QUESTION_TEXT: What is polymorphism? How is function overloading an example of polymorphism?
ANSWER_TEXT: Polymorphism means one name, multiple forms. Function overloading is compile-time polymorphism where multiple functions share the same name but differ in parameters.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to perform bubble sort on an array of n integers in ascending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: void bubbleSort(int a[], int n){ for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++) for(int j=0;j<n-i-1;j++) if(a[j]>a[j+1]){ int t=a[j]; a[j]=a[j+1]; a[j+1]=t; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: Calculate the address of element A[3][2] given base address 1000, 4 bytes per element, and 4 columns.
ANSWER_TEXT: Address = Base + (Row*Cols + Col)*Size = 1000 + (3*4+2)*4 = 1000 + 56 = 1056

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to search an element in a sorted array using binary search.
ANSWER_TEXT: int binarySearch(int a[], int n, int key){ int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<=r){ int m=(l+r)/2; if(a[m]==key) return m; else if(a[m]<key) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a binary file in C++? How does it differ from a text file?
ANSWER_TEXT: Binary files store data in binary format (as it is in memory). Text files store data in human-readable ASCII form. Binary files are faster and compact; text files are portable and editable.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ program to write an integer array to a binary file using write().
ANSWER_TEXT:
#include<fstream.h>
void main(){ int a[]={1,2,3,4,5}; ofstream f('data.dat',ios::binary); f.write((char*)a,sizeof(a)); f.close(); }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of seekg() and tellg() in C++ file handling?
TOPIC: C++ Binary File Handling / seekg / tellg
ANSWER_TEXT: seekg() moves the get pointer to a specified position. tellg() returns the current position of the get pointer in the file.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to count the number of objects stored in a binary file for a class EMPLOYEE.
ANSWER_TEXT: int countObjects(){ ifstream f('emp.dat',ios::binary); int cnt=0; EMPLOYEE e; while(f.read((char*)&e,sizeof(e))) cnt++; f.close(); return cnt; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to read and display all records from a text file 'students.txt' line by line.
ANSWER_TEXT: void display(){ ifstream f('students.txt'); char line[100]; while(f.getline(line,100)) cout<<line<<endl; f.close(); }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a function to insert a node at the beginning of a singly linked list in C++.
TOPIC: C++ Linked List / Queue / Dynamic Memory
ANSWER_TEXT: void insertAtFront(Node *&head, int data){ Node *n=new Node; n->data=data; n->next=head; head=n; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write C++ functions PUSH() and POP() for a stack implemented using an array. Assume MAX=10.
Convert the infix expression A+B*C-D to postfix using a stack. Show all steps.
ANSWER_TEXT: Steps: A→output, +→stack, B→output, *→stack(higher prec), C→output, pop * B*C, then -, pop + A+B*C, D→output, pop -. Postfix: ABC*+D-

QUESTION_TEXT: Evaluate the postfix expression 5 3 2 * + using a stack. Show steps.
ANSWER_TEXT: Push 5, push 3, push 2, * → 3*2=6 push 6, + → 5+6=11. Result: 11

QUESTION_TEXT: Identify which of the following are keywords and which are user-defined identifiers in C++: (i) float (ii) MyClass (iii) return (iv) counter99
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) float - Keyword (ii) MyClass - User defined Identifier (iii) return - Keyword (iv) counter99 - User defined Identifier

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between public and private access specifiers with an example in C++.

ANSWER_TEXT: Polymorphism means one name, multiple forms. Function overloading is compile-time polymorphism where multiple functions share the same name but differ in parameters.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to perform bubble sort on an array of n integers in ascending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: void bubbleSort(int a[], int n){ for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++) for(int j=0;j<n-i-1;j++) if(a[j]>a[j+1]){ int t=a[j]; a[j]=a[j+1]; a[j+1]=t; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: Calculate the address of element A[3][2] given base address 1000, 4 bytes per element, and 4 columns.
ANSWER_TEXT: Address = Base + (Row*Cols + Col)*Size = 1000 + (3*4+2)*4 = 1000 + 56 = 1056

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to search an element in a sorted array using binary search.
ANSWER_TEXT: int binarySearch(int a[], int n, int key){ int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<=r){ int m=(l+r)/2; if(a[m]==key) return m; else if(a[m]<key) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a binary file in C++? How does it differ from a text file?

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to perform bubble sort on an array of n integers in ascending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: void bubbleSort(int a[], int n){ for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++) for(int j=0;j<n-i-1;j++) if(a[j]>a[j+1]){ int t=a[j]; a[j]=a[j+1]; a[j+1]=t; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: Calculate the address of element A[3][2] given base address 1000, 4 bytes per element, and 4 columns.
ANSWER_TEXT: Address = Base + (Row*Cols + Col)*Size = 1000 + (3*4+2)*4 = 1000 + 56 = 1056

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to search an element in a sorted array using binary search.
ANSWER_TEXT: int binarySearch(int a[], int n, int key){ int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<=r){ int m=(l+r)/2; if(a[m]==key) return m; else if(a[m]<key) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a binary file in C++? How does it differ from a text file?

ANSWER_TEXT: Steps: A→output, +→stack, B→output, *→stack(higher prec), C→output, pop * B*C, then -, pop + A+B*C, D→output, pop -. Postfix: ABC*+D-

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a default argument in C++ function? Write a function Area() with one default argument.
TOPIC: C++ Basics - Reference Parameters and Default Arguments
ANSWER_TEXT: A default argument provides a default value if no value is passed. Example: float Area(float l, float b=5.0){ return l*b; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function using #define to find the cube of a number. Show its usage in main().
ANSWER_TEXT:
#define CUBE(x) (x*x*x)
void main(){ int n=3; cout<<CUBE(n); }  Output: 27

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the output of: int arr[]={5,10,15,20}; int *p=arr; cout<<*p<<' '<<*(p+2)<<' '<<p[3];
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: 5 15 20

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of function overloading in C++ with an example showing two functions named calculate().
ANSWER_TEXT: Function overloading allows multiple functions with the same name but different parameters. Example: int calculate(int a, int b){ return a+b; } float calculate(float a, float b){ return a*b; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ program to find the largest element in a 1D array of n integers using a function.
ANSWER_TEXT: int largest(int arr[], int n){ int max=arr[0]; for(int i=1;i<n;i++) if(arr[i]>max) max=arr[i]; return max; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to calculate the sum of each row of a 2D array of size 3x3.
ANSWER_TEXT: void rowSum(int a[][3], int r){ for(int i=0;i<r;i++){ int s=0; for(int j=0;j<3;j++) s+=a[i][j]; cout<<'Row '<<i<<' sum='<<s<<endl; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the address stored in pointer p? int arr[5]={10,20,30,40,50}; int *p=arr+2; cout<<*p;
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: 30 (p points to arr[2] which is 30)

QUESTION_TEXT: What will be output of: for(int i=0;i<3;i++){ for(int j=i;j<3;j++) cout<<'*'; cout<<endl; }
Explain the use of random() function in C++ and write a statement to generate a random number between 1 and 100.
ANSWER_TEXT: random() generates a pseudo-random number. Statement: int r = random(100)+1;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is encapsulation in C++? How is it achieved?
ANSWER_TEXT: Encapsulation is the binding of data and functions together. It is achieved using classes where data members are private and accessed through public member functions.

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between a constructor and destructor in C++ with examples.
ANSWER_TEXT: Constructor: initializes object on creation (e.g., Test(){ x=0; }). Destructor: cleans up on object destruction (e.g., ~Test(){ cout<<"Destroyed"; }).

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ class STUDENT with private members RollNo and Name, and public members to input and display data.
ANSWER_TEXT: class STUDENT{ int RollNo; char Name[30]; public: void GetData(){ cin>>RollNo>>Name; } void ShowData(){ cout<<RollNo<<' '<<Name; } };

QUESTION_TEXT: What is inheritance? List the types of inheritance supported in C++ with one-line descriptions.
ANSWER_TEXT: Inheritance allows a derived class to reuse properties of base class. Types: Single, Multiple, Multilevel, Hierarchical, Hybrid inheritance.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ class hierarchy showing single inheritance: class Vehicle as base and class Car as derived. Include one data member each.
ANSWER_TEXT: class Vehicle{ protected: int speed; public: void setSpeed(int s){ speed=s; } }; class Car:public Vehicle{ int doors; public: void setDoors(int d){ doors=d; } void show(){ cout<<speed<<' '<<doors; } };

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a copy constructor? Write one for a class BOOK with a single integer data member Pages.
ANSWER_TEXT: BOOK(BOOK &b){ Pages=b.Pages; } Copy constructor creates a new object as a copy of an existing object.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the significance of the scope resolution operator (::) in C++?
ANSWER_TEXT: The :: operator is used to define member functions outside the class and to access global variables when a local variable has the same name.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write the output: class A{ public: A(){ cout<<'A'; } ~A(){ cout<<'~A'; } }; void main(){ A obj1, obj2; }
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: AA~A~A (constructors called in order, destructors in reverse)

QUESTION_TEXT: Differentiate between public and private access specifiers with an example in C++.
ANSWER_TEXT: Private members cannot be accessed from outside the class. Public members can be accessed from anywhere. Example: class X{ int priv; public: int pub; };

QUESTION_TEXT: What is polymorphism? How is function overloading an example of polymorphism?
ANSWER_TEXT: Polymorphism means one name, multiple forms. Function overloading is compile-time polymorphism where multiple functions share the same name but differ in parameters.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to perform bubble sort on an array of n integers in ascending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: void bubbleSort(int a[], int n){ for(int i=0;i<n-1;i++) for(int j=0;j<n-i-1;j++) if(a[j]>a[j+1]){ int t=a[j]; a[j]=a[j+1]; a[j+1]=t; } }

QUESTION_TEXT: Calculate the address of element A[3][2] given base address 1000, 4 bytes per element, and 4 columns.
ANSWER_TEXT: Address = Base + (Row*Cols + Col)*Size = 1000 + (3*4+2)*4 = 1000 + 56 = 1056

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to search an element in a sorted array using binary search.
ANSWER_TEXT: int binarySearch(int a[], int n, int key){ int l=0,r=n-1; while(l<=r){ int m=(l+r)/2; if(a[m]==key) return m; else if(a[m]<key) l=m+1; else r=m-1; } return -1; }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a binary file in C++? How does it differ from a text file?
ANSWER_TEXT: Binary files store data in binary format (as it is in memory). Text files store data in human-readable ASCII form. Binary files are faster and compact; text files are portable and editable.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ program to write an integer array to a binary file using write().
ANSWER_TEXT:
#include<fstream.h>
void main(){ int a[]={1,2,3,4,5}; ofstream f('data.dat',ios::binary); f.write((char*)a,sizeof(a)); f.close(); }

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of seekg() and tellg() in C++ file handling?
TOPIC: C++ Binary File Handling / seekg / tellg
ANSWER_TEXT: seekg() moves the get pointer to a specified position. tellg() returns the current position of the get pointer in the file.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to count the number of objects stored in a binary file for a class EMPLOYEE.
ANSWER_TEXT: int countObjects(){ ifstream f('emp.dat',ios::binary); int cnt=0; EMPLOYEE e; while(f.read((char*)&e,sizeof(e))) cnt++; f.close(); return cnt; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a C++ function to read and display all records from a text file 'students.txt' line by line.
ANSWER_TEXT: void display(){ ifstream f('students.txt'); char line[100]; while(f.getline(line,100)) cout<<line<<endl; f.close(); }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a function to insert a node at the beginning of a singly linked list in C++.
TOPIC: C++ Linked List / Queue / Dynamic Memory
ANSWER_TEXT: void insertAtFront(Node *&head, int data){ Node *n=new Node; n->data=data; n->next=head; head=n; }

QUESTION_TEXT: Write C++ functions PUSH() and POP() for a stack implemented using an array. Assume MAX=10.
Convert the infix expression A+B*C-D to postfix using a stack. Show all steps.
ANSWER_TEXT: Steps: A→output, +→stack, B→output, *→stack(higher prec), C→output, pop * B*C, then -, pop + A+B*C, D→output, pop -. Postfix: ABC*+D-

QUESTION_TEXT: Evaluate the postfix expression 5 3 2 * + using a stack. Show steps.
ANSWER_TEXT: Push 5, push 3, push 2, * → 3*2=6 push 6, + → 5+6=11. Result: 11

QUESTION_TEXT: Identify which of the following are keywords and which are user-defined identifiers in C++: (i) float (ii) MyClass (iii) return (iv) counter99
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) float - Keyword (ii) MyClass - User defined Identifier (iii) return - Keyword (iv) counter99 - User defined Identifier

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between a keyword and an identifier in C++? Give one example of each.
ANSWER_TEXT: Keywords are reserved words with predefined meaning in C++ (e.g., int, while). Identifiers are user-defined names for variables, functions, etc. (e.g., total, myFunc).

QUESTION_TEXT: Rewrite the following C++ code removing errors: void main(){ int A,B; cin>>A,B; if A>B cout<<A; else cout<<B; }
ANSWER_TEXT: Errors: (1) cin>>A,B; should be cin>>A>>B; (2) Missing parentheses in if condition. Corrected: if(A>B) cout<<A; else cout<<B;

QUESTION_TEXT: Find the output of the following C++ code: int a=5,b=3; cout<<a++<<' '<<++b<<' '<<a+b;
ANSWER_TEXT: Output: 5 4 10 (a++ gives 5 then becomes 6, ++b gives 4, a+b = 6+4 = 10)

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between call by value and call by reference in C++? Illustrate with an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: Call by value: a copy of the argument is passed; changes do not affect original. Call by reference: address is passed; changes affect original. Example: void swap(int &a, int &b){ int t=a; a=b; b=t; }

What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks BETWEEN 60 AND 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING clause in SQL?
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display all records from EMPLOYEES table where name starts with letter 'A'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks BETWEEN 60 AND 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING clause in SQL?
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display all records from EMPLOYEES table where name starts with letter 'A'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).

TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display all records from EMPLOYEES table where name starts with letter 'A'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks BETWEEN 60 AND 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING clause in SQL?
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display all records from EMPLOYEES table where name starts with letter 'A'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks BETWEEN 60 AND 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING clause in SQL?
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display all records from EMPLOYEES table where name starts with letter 'A'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks BETWEEN 60 AND 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING clause in SQL?
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all products with a price between 100 and 500, excluding those in category 'Electronics'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM PRODUCTS WHERE Price BETWEEN 100 AND 500 AND Category != 'Electronics';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is referential integrity? How is it maintained using foreign keys?
ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

ANSWER_TEXT: Referential integrity ensures that a foreign key value must match an existing primary key in the referenced table. It prevents orphan records.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display all records from EMPLOYEES table where name starts with letter 'A'.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT * FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a primary key in a relational database? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table. Example: RollNo in a STUDENTS table.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between DELETE and DROP commands in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE removes rows from a table (DML). DROP removes the entire table structure from the database (DDL).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of the WHERE clause in SQL? Write a query to find all students with marks > 80.
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows based on condition. Query: SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 80;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names and salaries of employees earning more than 50000, ordered by salary in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, Salary FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary > 50000 ORDER BY Salary DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to find the total, average, maximum, and minimum marks of students in each class.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Class, SUM(Marks), AVG(Marks), MAX(Marks), MIN(Marks) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Class;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a foreign key? How does it enforce referential integrity?
ANSWER_TEXT: A foreign key is a column that references the primary key of another table. It enforces referential integrity by ensuring values in the foreign key exist in the referenced primary key.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to display the names of students who have scored marks between 60 and 90 (inclusive).
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks BETWEEN 60 AND 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING clause in SQL?
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY is applied.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query to count the number of employees in each department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write an SQL query using JOIN to display student name and their course name from STUDENTS and COURSES tables.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT S.Name, C.CourseName FROM STUDENTS S JOIN COURSES C ON S.CourseID = C.CourseID;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN in SQL? Give an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: INNER JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables. LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right. Example: SELECT * FROM A LEFT JOIN B ON A.id=B.id;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display distinct city names from the CUSTOMERS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT DISTINCT City FROM CUSTOMERS;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write SQL query to add a new column 'Grade' of type VARCHAR(2) to the STUDENTS table.
ANSWER_TEXT: ALTER TABLE STUDENTS ADD Grade VARCHAR(2);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to delete all records from ORDERS table where OrderDate is before '2020-01-01'.
ANSWER_TEXT: DELETE FROM ORDERS WHERE OrderDate < '2020-01-01';

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to update the department of employee with EmpID=101 to 'HR'.
ANSWER_TEXT: UPDATE EMPLOYEES SET Department='HR' WHERE EmpID=101;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the use of GROUP BY clause in SQL? Write a query to find the number of students in each city.
TOPIC: SQL - GROUP BY and Aggregate Functions
ANSWER_TEXT: GROUP BY groups rows with same values. Query: SELECT City, COUNT(*) FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY City;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display names of all employees who do NOT work in the 'Sales' department.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Department != 'Sales';

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a view in SQL? Write a statement to create a view TOPSTUDENTS showing students with marks > 90.
ANSWER_TEXT: A view is a virtual table based on a SELECT query. CREATE VIEW TOPSTUDENTS AS SELECT * FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > 90;

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the second highest salary from an EMPLOYEES table.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES WHERE Salary < (SELECT MAX(Salary) FROM EMPLOYEES);

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of normalization in RDBMS. What is the purpose of 1NF?
ANSWER_TEXT: Normalization organizes tables to reduce redundancy. 1NF ensures each column contains atomic (indivisible) values and each row is unique.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query using HAVING to find departments with more than 5 employees.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Department, COUNT(*) FROM EMPLOYEES GROUP BY Department HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a subquery? Write a SQL query to find names of students who scored above the average marks.
ANSWER_TEXT: A subquery is a query inside another query. SELECT Name FROM STUDENTS WHERE Marks > (SELECT AVG(Marks) FROM STUDENTS);

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to find all customers from 'Delhi' who placed orders in 2023.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT C.Name FROM CUSTOMERS C JOIN ORDERS O ON C.CustID=O.CustID WHERE C.City='Delhi' AND YEAR(O.OrderDate)=2023;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between CHAR and VARCHAR data types in SQL?
ANSWER_TEXT: CHAR stores fixed-length strings (padded with spaces). VARCHAR stores variable-length strings (uses only needed space). VARCHAR is more storage-efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: Write a SQL query to display the names of students along with their total marks, sorted in descending order.
ANSWER_TEXT: SELECT Name, SUM(Marks) AS Total FROM STUDENTS GROUP BY Name ORDER BY Total DESC;

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between a hub and a switch?
ANSWER_TEXT: Hub broadcasts data to all devices; switch sends data only to the intended device using MAC addresses. Switch is more efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the full form of: (i) HTTP (ii) FTP (iii) SMTP (iv) DNS?
ANSWER_TEXT: (i) HyperText Transfer Protocol (ii) File Transfer Protocol (iii) Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (iv) Domain Name System

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between LAN and WAN?
ANSWER_TEXT: LAN (Local Area Network) covers a small area like a building. WAN (Wide Area Network) covers a large geographic area like a country or continent.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is IP addressing? What is the difference between IPv4 and IPv6?
ANSWER_TEXT: IP address uniquely identifies a device on a network. IPv4: 32-bit, dotted decimal (e.g., 192.168.1.1). IPv6: 128-bit, hexadecimal, supports more addresses.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a firewall? What is its role in network security?
ANSWER_TEXT: A firewall monitors and controls incoming/outgoing network traffic based on security rules. It protects a network from unauthorized access.

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the client-server model with an example.
ANSWER_TEXT: In client-server model, the server provides resources/services and the client requests them. Example: Web browser (client) requests a webpage from a web server.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between a router and a modem?
ANSWER_TEXT: Modem converts digital data to analog (for transmission). Router routes data packets between networks. A modem connects to ISP; router connects devices within a network.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is WiFi? Which IEEE standard defines WiFi?
ANSWER_TEXT: WiFi is wireless networking technology. It is defined by IEEE 802.11 standards for wireless LANs.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is a MAC address? How is it different from an IP address?
ANSWER_TEXT: MAC address: hardware address burned into the NIC (permanent). IP address: logical address assigned by network (can change). MAC = Layer 2; IP = Layer 3.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between a Hacker and a Cracker?
ANSWER_TEXT: Hacker explores systems to improve security (ethical). Cracker breaks into systems with malicious intent. Crackers are criminals; ethical hackers are security professionals.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is topology? Compare star and bus topologies.
ANSWER_TEXT: Topology is the layout of a network. Star: all devices connect to a central switch/hub (easier to manage, single point of failure). Bus: all devices share one backbone cable (cheaper, harder to troubleshoot).

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the purpose of DNS in a network?
ANSWER_TEXT: DNS (Domain Name System) translates domain names (e.g., www.google.com) to IP addresses, enabling browsers to find websites.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is cybercrime? Name any two types of cybercrime.
ANSWER_TEXT: Cybercrime is illegal activity involving computers or the internet. Types: (1) Phishing - stealing credentials via fake websites. (2) Ransomware - encrypting files for ransom.

QUESTION_TEXT: Suggest a network topology for a school with 50 computers in 5 labs. Justify your choice.
ANSWER_TEXT: Star topology is ideal. Each lab has a switch; all switches connect to a central router. Easy to add/remove computers, fault is isolated to one segment.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is cloud computing? Name two service models of cloud computing.
ANSWER_TEXT: Cloud computing delivers computing services over the internet. Service models: (1) IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) (2) SaaS (Software as a Service)

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the role of a repeater in a network?
ANSWER_TEXT: A repeater regenerates and amplifies weak network signals to extend the range of a network segment.

QUESTION_TEXT: What are cookies in web browsing? What is their purpose?
ANSWER_TEXT: Cookies are small data files stored by a browser on a user's computer. They remember user preferences, login sessions, and browsing history.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the difference between circuit switching and packet switching?
ANSWER_TEXT: Circuit switching: dedicated path established for entire communication (e.g., telephone). Packet switching: data split into packets sent independently (e.g., internet). Packet switching is more efficient.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the OSI model? Name its seven layers.
ANSWER_TEXT: OSI (Open Systems Interconnection) model has 7 layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is HTTPS? How does it differ from HTTP?
ANSWER_TEXT: HTTPS is HTTP with SSL/TLS encryption. It provides secure, encrypted communication. HTTP is unsecured; HTTPS protects data during transmission.

QUESTION_TEXT: What is the significance of subnetting in IP networking?
ANSWER_TEXT: Subnetting divides a large network into smaller subnetworks (subnets), improving performance, security, and efficient IP address utilization.

QUESTION_TEXT: Explain the concept of bandwidth and its importance in networking.
ANSWER_TEXT: Bandwidth is the maximum data transfer rate of a network. Higher bandwidth means faster data transmission. Measured in bps, Kbps, Mbps, or Gbps.
